Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia, low HbA1c, elevated creatinine (suggesting impaired renal function), and mild metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance/color, indicating possible nephropathy and metabolic derangement. No evidence of urinary infection. Findings are suggestive of renal impairment with possible underlying diabetes and/or liver dysfunction; clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.
